generated_comment: Bro honestly in most homes jo jahan better hai wahi handle karta hai. Guys usually deal with money, repairs, outside work etc, and girls are just better at managing kitchen and home stuff, so why force 50-50 for everything? If the system is working, it's practical only.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it naturalizes a gendered division of labor as efficiency rather than bias. It encodes the 'jo jahan better hai' practicality frame and assumes women are inherently suited to domestic work while men handle finances and external tasks.
original_frame: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
persona_text: You are Arjun. Age 23. Single. A college student in Bengaluru. You are in a WhatsApp group with seven male college friends. Someone has shared a meme that says women spend two hours putting on makeup and then get angry when men call them fake. You are reacting to the meme and adding your own comment about women who wear heavy makeup and what they are hiding and whether men have a right to know what a woman actually looks like before committing to a relationship.
